items.title: [正面] Gameplay is fun but they really need to do a prope...
items.summary:
Gameplay is fun but they really need to do a proper translation. Card names, keywords and everything is translated with different words across the game and it can be confusing many times,

Generous gacha at least at the beginning that I played.

items.title: [正面] tldr:  Surprisingly good for a budget indie studio...
items.summary:
tldr:  Surprisingly good for a budget indie studio.  The darker, eldritch sibling of Reverse 1999.

Main pros:
-Art style goes hard.  #1 reason I tried it
-GREAT story and worldbuilding which sticks to its Lovecraftian themes
-Good roguelite gameplay and team building variety
-No stupid RGB element systems I see everywhere, kudos for sticking out
-Gacha can be mostly ignored if you just want the story, but game is VERY generous

Mostly minor cons:
-EN translation is GPT generated to the point it degrades story experience
-Rough intro (bugged a bit?)
-Gacha (look, I get it -- hosting a casino makes money, but that doesn't make it good)
-Story details omitted/fail to get through to reader sometimes

Scattered thoughts drifting on the net:
Whereas R:1999 is to cultural and nerd references/trivia which leads to the setting being a bit of a mishmash at times, Morimens digs deep into its gothic/horror roots and commits everything to it.  The art team has a cinematic eye and know how to frame a narrative.  Despite the translation issues, I found myself caring for the characters and playing through CH 1-2 just for the story alone.  The writing itself has a nice, emotional flair interspersed with humor. 

Gameplay hits a sweet spot with varied challenges for stages which change the way you build your team and play with them.  4 team archetypes exist which you can partially mix-n-match.  The game also doesn't hold your hand -- It can be difficult!

It's worth trying an hour or two just to check out the art.

items.title: [正面] Esta bueno, se entiende relativamente fácil y tien...
items.summary: Esta bueno, se entiende relativamente fácil y tiene sus parecidos a reverse pero con sus propias diferencias que lo hace ( único ) sobre todo el combate siento que si es un poco mas estrategico
